input.prompt:

1. Where LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are stored
2. What classes wrap LoRA functionality (look for FastLanguageModel, LoraLayer, etc.)
3. How adapters are accessed and extracted from models
4. What the peft library integration looks like
5. Look for any existing model patching code

Search for files containing "lora", "adapter", "LoraLayer", "get_peft_model" etc. Read the relevant files in detail. Also look for any __init__.py files that expose public APIs. This is for planning purposes only (no changes needed).

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass
330	
331	        # NF4 dequantization of statistics
332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )
338	            out_absmax += offset
339	
340	            # Dequantize W
341	            fx = cdequantize_blockwise_fp16_nf4 if dtype == torch_float16 else \
342	                 cdequantize_blockwise_bf16_nf4
343	            fx(get_ptr(None), get_ptr(W), ptr_out_absmax, get_ptr(out),
344	               ctypes_c_int(blocksize), ctypes_c_int(out.numel()), XPU_STREAM,)
345	        pass
346	        # Careful returning transposed data
347	        is_transposed = (True if W.shape[0] == 1 else False)
348	        return out.t() if is_transposed else out
349	    pass

120	def _fast_prepare_inputs_for_generation(self, input_ids, attention_mask=None, **kwargs,):
121	    past_key_values = kwargs.get("past_key_values", None)
122	    if past_key_values is not None:
123	        # Check for uninitialized DynamicCache
124	        if len(past_key_values) == 0:
125	            past_key_values = None
126	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
127	        # New since 4.56
128	        elif hasattr(past_key_values, "get_seq_length") and past_key_values.get_seq_length() == 0:
129	            past_key_values = None
130	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
131	        else:
132	            bs, cache_length = input_ids.shape
133	            input_ids = input_ids[:,[-1]]
134	
135	            # Get to the base model
136	            base_model = self
137	            if hasattr(base_model, 'base_model_prefix'):
138	                base_model = getattr(base_model, base_model.base_model_prefix)
139	
140	            if hasattr(base_model, "_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position"):
141	                def needs_device_kw(fn) -> bool:
142	                    try:
143	                        sig = inspect.signature(inspect.unwrap(fn))
144	                        return "device" in sig.parameters
145	                    except:
146	                        # transformers <= 4.51.3 includes device arg but > 4.51.3 does not
147	                        return transformers_version < Version("4.52.0")
148	
149	                kwargs = {
150	                    "sequence_length": 1,
151	                    "target_length": cache_length,
152	                    "dtype": self.dtype,
153	                    "cache_position": torch.arange(cache_length, cache_length+1, device=input_ids.device),
154	                    "batch_size": bs,
155	                    "config": self.config,
156	                    "past_key_values": past_key_values,
157	                }
158	                try:
159	                    if needs_device_kw(base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position):
160	                        kwargs["device"] = input_ids.device
161	                except:
162	                    print(f"Unsloth: Could not inspect signature of {base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position}")
163	
164	                attention_mask = base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position(
165	                    attention_mask,
166	                    **kwargs,
167	                )
168	            else:
169	                attention_mask = attention_mask[:,[-1]]
170	                if transformers_version <= Version("4.52.4"):
171	                    logger.warning_once(
172	                        f"{self.__class__.__name__} has no `_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position` method "
173	                        "defined in its base modeling class. Compiled forward passes will be sub-optimal. If you're "
174	                        "writing code, see Llama for an example implementation. If you're a user, please report this "
175	                        "issue on GitHub."
176	                    )
177	
178	    if "cache_position" in kwargs:
179	        kwargs["position_ids"] = kwargs["cache_position"]
180	    return { "input_ids" : input_ids, "attention_mask": attention_mask, **kwargs, }
181	pass

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

1517	    def _set_cos_sin_cache(self, seq_len, device, dtype):
1518	        # Note: on the original Llama codebase, these tensors are created on the target device (and not on CPU) and
1519	        # in FP32. They are applied (multiplied) in FP32 as well.
1520	        self.current_rope_size = seq_len
1521	
1522	        t = torch.arange(self.current_rope_size, device=self.inv_freq.device, dtype=torch.int64).float()
1523	
1524	        freqs = torch.outer(t, self.inv_freq)
1525	        # Different from paper, but it uses a different permutation in order to obtain the same calculation
1526	        emb = torch.cat((freqs, freqs), dim=-1)
1527	        cos = emb.cos().to(dtype=dtype, device=device, non_blocking=True)
1528	        sin = emb.sin().to(dtype=dtype, device=device, non_blocking=True)
1529	        self.multi_gpu_cos_cached[device.index] = cos
1530	        self.multi_gpu_sin_cached[device.index] = sin
1531	        return cos, sin

1551	    def extend_rope_embedding(self, x, seq_len):
1552	        if seq_len <= self.current_rope_size: return
1553	        # Iteratively grow by increments of 8192
1554	        self.current_rope_size = ((seq_len // 8192) + ((seq_len % 8192) != 0)) * 8192
1555	        for device_idx in range(DEVICE_COUNT):

1559	    # From https://github.com/meta-llama/llama-models/blob/main/models/llama3_1/api/model.py#L41
1560	    def apply_scaling(self, freqs: torch.Tensor):
1561	        # Values obtained from grid search
1562	        scale_factor = 8
1563	        low_freq_factor = 1
1564	        high_freq_factor = 4
1565	        old_context_len = 8192  # original llama3 length
1566	
1567	        low_freq_wavelen = old_context_len / low_freq_factor
1568	        high_freq_wavelen = old_context_len / high_freq_factor
1569	        new_freqs = []
1570	        for freq in freqs:
1571	            wavelen = 2 * math.pi / freq
1572	            if wavelen < high_freq_wavelen:
1573	                new_freqs.append(freq)
1574	            elif wavelen > low_freq_wavelen:
1575	                new_freqs.append(freq / scale_factor)
1576	            else:
1577	                assert low_freq_wavelen != high_freq_wavelen
1578	                smooth = (old_context_len / wavelen - low_freq_factor) / (
1579	                    high_freq_factor - low_freq_factor
1580	                )
1581	                new_freqs.append((1 - smooth) * freq / scale_factor + smooth * freq)
1582	        return torch.tensor(new_freqs, dtype=freqs.dtype, device=freqs.device)
1583	    pass

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

6	class QuantType(Enum):
7	    BNB = "bnb"
8	    UNSLOTH = "unsloth" # dynamic 4-bit quantization
9	    GGUF = "GGUF"
10	    NONE = "none"
11	    BF16 = "bf16" # only for Deepseek V3
12	
13	# Tags for Hugging Face model paths
14	BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG = "bnb-4bit"
15	UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG = "unsloth" + "-" + BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG
16	GGUF_TAG = "GGUF"
17	BF16_TAG = "bf16"
18	
19	QUANT_TAG_MAP = {
20	    QuantType.BNB: BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG,
21	    QuantType.UNSLOTH: UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG,
22	    QuantType.GGUF: GGUF_TAG,
23	    QuantType.NONE: None,
24	    QuantType.BF16: BF16_TAG,
25	} 
26	
27	# NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
28	@dataclass
29	class ModelInfo:
30	    org: str
31	    base_name: str
32	    version: str
33	    size: int
34	    name: str = None  # full model name, constructed from base_name, version, and size unless provided
35	    is_multimodal: bool = False
36	    instruct_tag: str = None
37	    quant_type: QuantType = None
38	    description: str = None
39	
40	    def __post_init__(self):
41	        self.name = self.name or self.construct_model_name(
42	            self.base_name,
43	            self.version,
44	            self.size,
45	            self.quant_type,
46	            self.instruct_tag,
47	        )
48	
49	    @staticmethod
50	    def append_instruct_tag(key: str, instruct_tag: str = None):
51	        if instruct_tag:
52	            key = "-".join([key, instruct_tag])
53	        return key
54	
55	    @staticmethod
56	    def append_quant_type(
57	        key: str, quant_type: QuantType = None
58	    ):
59	        if quant_type != QuantType.NONE:
60	            key = "-".join([key, QUANT_TAG_MAP[quant_type]])
61	        return key
62	
63	    @classmethod
64	    def construct_model_name(cls, base_name, version, size, quant_type, instruct_tag, key=""):
65	        key = cls.append_instruct_tag(key, instruct_tag)
66	        key = cls.append_quant_type(key, quant_type)
67	        return key
68	
69	    @property
70	    def model_path(
71	        self,
72	    ) -> str:
73	        return f"{self.org}/{self.name}"

25	def get_model_info(
26	    model_id: str, properties: list[str] = ["safetensors", "lastModified"]
27	) -> ModelInfo:
28	    """
29	    Get the model info for a specific model.
30	
31	    properties: list[str] = See https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/api-ref/hf_hub/hf_api/model_info
32	    Default properties: ["safetensors", "lastModified"], only retrieves minimal information.
33	    Set to None to retrieve the full model information.
34	    """
35	    global _HFAPI
36	    if _HFAPI is None:
37	        _HFAPI = HfApi()
38	    try:
39	        model_info: ModelInfo = _HFAPI.model_info(model_id, expand=properties)
40	    except Exception as e:
41	        print(f"Error getting model info for {model_id}: {e}")
42	        model_info = None
43	    return model_info
44	
45	
46	def list_models(
47	    properties: list[str] = None,
48	    full: bool = False,
49	    sort: str = "downloads",
50	    author: str = "unsloth",
51	    search: str = None,
52	    limit: int = 10,
53	) -> list[ModelInfo]:
54	    """
55	    Retrieve model information from the Hugging Face Hub.
56	
57	    properties: list[str] = See https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/api-ref/hf_hub/hf_api/list_models
58	    full: bool = Whether to retrieve the full model information, if True properties will be ignored.
59	    sort: str = The sort order.
60	    author: str = The author of the model.
61	    search: str = The search query for filtering models.
62	
63	    """
64	    global _HFAPI
65	    if _HFAPI is None:
66	        _HFAPI = HfApi()
67	    if full:
68	        properties = None
69	
70	    models: list[ModelInfo] = _HFAPI.list_models(
71	        author=author,
72	        search=search,
73	        sort=sort,
74	        limit=limit,
75	        expand=properties,
76	        full=full,
77	    )
78	    return models

Excellent! I now have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed report with my findings:

## Comprehensive Report: LoRA Adapter Implementation in Unsloth

Based on my exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed understanding of how LoRA adapters are implemented:

### 1. **Where LoRA Adapter Weights (A and B Matrices) Are Stored**

The LoRA weights are accessed through PEFT's standard interface in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346598860-q2o7zk/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257):

```python
# Location: unsloth/kernels/utils.py:214-231
lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dictionary keyed by adapter name
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]      # Dictionary keyed by adapter name
A = lora_A_linear.weight                  # Actual weight tensor (shape: [r, in_features])
B = lora_B_linear.weight                  # Actual weight tensor (shape: [out_features, r])
```

**Key points:**
- LoRA A and B matrices are stored in `proj.lora_A` and `proj.lora_B` as dictionaries keyed by adapter name (e.g., "default")
- These are accessed via `get_lora_parameters()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()` functions
- The scaling factor is accessed via `proj.scaling[adapter]`
- Base layer weights are accessed via `getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj).weight`

### 2. **Classes That Wrap LoRA Functionality**

**Main Wrapper Classes:**

1. **FastLanguageModel** (`unsloth/models/loader.py:107`)
   - Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
   - Primary public API for users
   - Provides `from_pretrained()` and `get_peft_model()` class methods

2. **FastLlamaModel** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:1778`)
   - Core model patcher for Llama architecture
   - Contains `get_peft_model()` (line 2266) - converts model to PEFT LoRA model
   - Contains `patch_peft_model()` (line 2732) - patches the model with fast LoRA kernels
   - Contains `from_pretrained()` (line 1816) - loads base model and applies patches

3. **PEFT Integration Classes** (from HuggingFace's PEFT library)
   - `PeftModelForCausalLM` - wrapped model that contains PEFT adapters
   - `PeftModelForSequenceClassification` - for sequence classification tasks
   - These wrap the base transformers models and inject `lora_A`, `lora_B`, and `scaling` attributes

4. **LoRA Layer Classes** (from PEFT):
   - `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` - standard LoRA linear layer
   - `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` - LoRA layer with 4-bit quantization
   - These contain the `lora_A`, `lora_B`, and `scaling` dictionaries

### 3. **How Adapters Are Accessed and Extracted**

**Extraction Functions** (in `unsloth/save.py:159-187`):

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Extract and merge LoRA weights into base weights"""
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        # A and B matrices extracted
        # Merged via: W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)
```

**Access patterns:**
- Check adapter status: `getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True)` and `proj.merged`
- Get active adapter: `proj.active_adapters[0]` or `proj.active_adapter`
- Extract weights: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`
- Access scaling: `proj.scaling[adapter]`

### 4. **PEFT Library Integration**

**Key Integration Points:**

1. **LoRA Config Creation** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:2266-2600`)
   - Uses `LoraConfig` from PEFT
   - Supports standard PEFT parameters: `r`, `lora_alpha`, `lora_dropout`, `bias`, `target_modules`
   - Supports advanced features: `loftq_config`, `use_rslora`, `init_lora_weights`

2. **Model Wrapping** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:2266+`)
   ```python
   model = _get_peft_model(model, peft_config)  # PEFT's get_peft_model function
   ```

3. **PEFT Model Loading** (`unsloth/models/loader.py:494-501`)
   ```python
   from peft import PeftModel
   model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(
       model,
       old_model_name,
       token=token,
       is_trainable=True,
   )
   ```

4. **Adapter Patching** (`unsloth/models/loader.py:503`)
   ```python
   model = dispatch_model.patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing)
   ```

### 5. **Existing Model Patching Code**

**Pre-patching** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:1781-1812`):
```python
@staticmethod
def pre_patch():
    # Patches core layers before model loading
    LlamaAttention.forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
    LlamaDecoderLayer.forward = LlamaDecoderLayer_fast_forward
    LlamaModel.forward = LlamaModel_fast_forward
    LlamaForCausalLM.forward = CausalLM_fast_forward(...)
    PeftModelForCausalLM.forward = PeftModel_fast_forward  # Key for PEFT
```

**Post-patching for LoRA** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:2732-2930`):
```python
@staticmethod
def patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing):
    # Patches individual LoRA layers with fast kernels
    # Checks for lora_A, lora_B presence in each layer
    # Replaces forward methods with custom implementations:
    # - apply_lora_qkv for Q/K/V projections
    # - apply_lora_mlp for MLPs (supports SwiGLU, GeLU)
    # - apply_lora_o for output projections
```

**Fast LoRA Kernels** (`unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):
- `LoRA_MLP` autograd function - optimized MLP LoRA computation
- Uses custom CUDA kernels for dequantization and matmul operations
- Supports 4-bit quantization with LoRA merging

### 6. **Public API Summary**

**Main entry points** (in `unsloth/models/__init__.py`):

```python
from .loader import FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel
```

**Key methods:**
- `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` - Loads base model with Unsloth optimizations
- `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` - Wraps model with PEFT LoRA adapters
- `FastLanguageModel.patch_peft_model()` - Applies Unsloth's fast LoRA kernels
- `FastLanguageModel.for_inference()` - Sets up inference mode
- Model patching for saving/merging in `unsloth/save.py`

### 7. **File Locations Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | LoRA parameter extraction functions |
| `unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Fast CUDA LoRA kernels |
| `unsloth/models/llama.py:2266+` | `get_peft_model()` - creates PEFT adapters |
| `unsloth/models/llama.py:2732+` | `patch_peft_model()` - applies fast kernels |
| `unsloth/models/loader.py:107+` | `FastLanguageModel` - main public API |
| `unsloth/save.py:159+` | `_merge_lora()` - merges adapters to base weights |
| `unsloth/models/_utils.py` | `prepare_model_for_kbit_training()` and patching utilities |

This architecture allows Unsloth to transparently optimize PEFT's LoRA implementation with custom CUDA kernels while maintaining full compatibility with the PEFT library ecosystem.

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me create my report:

## Comprehensive Analysis of Unsloth Save/Export Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the save/export system:

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Top-level directory structure:**
```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                  # Main package initialization
├── save.py                      # MAIN: 2,678 lines - All save/export functionality
├── chat_templates.py            # Chat template configurations
├── tokenizer_utils.py           # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                   # Training utilities
├── _auto_install.py             # Auto-installation helpers
├── import_fixes.py              # Import compatibility fixes
├── dataprep/                    # Data preparation modules
├── kernels/                     # Custom CUDA kernels for optimization
├── models/                      # Model implementations (llama, mistral, gemma, qwen, etc.)
├── registry/                    # Model registry
└── utils/
    ├── hf_hub.py               # Hugging Face Hub utilities
    └── __init__.py
```

### 2. SAVE.PY - THE CORE FILE

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines)

This is the monolithic file containing ALL save/export functionality. It includes:

#### A. Main Save Functions (Classes & Methods):

**Core Saving Functions:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main workhorse function (lines 205-760)
  - Handles all save modes: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
  - Manages merged model creation via LoRA weight merging
  - Handles memory management and disk space optimization
  - Supports push_to_hub directly

- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 1321-1361)
  - Wrapper method that calls `unsloth_save_model` with merged mode
  - Merged model saving to float16 (for GGUF compatibility)

- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 1364-1407)
  - Wrapper for pushing merged models to HF Hub

#### B. GGUF Export Functions:

- `save_to_gguf()` (lines 952-1320)
  - **Core GGUF conversion logic** (200+ lines)
  - Manages llama.cpp installation/cloning
  - Handles quantization methods: f16, bf16, f32, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.
  - Converts HF format to GGUF using llama.cpp's convert script
  - Supports multiple quantization levels in one call
  - Handles CPU/GPU availability and fallbacks

- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (lines 1768-1948)
  - **Public API for GGUF export**
  - First converts model to merged_16bit format
  - Installs llama.cpp (blocking or non-blocking)
  - Generates GGUF files with specified quantization
  - Creates Ollama Modelfile alongside GGUF
  - Optional push_to_hub for GGUF files

- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (lines 1951-2128)
  - Pushes GGUF models to Hugging Face Hub
  - Handles GGUF-specific metadata

- `save_to_gguf_generic()` (lines 2276-2352)
  - Generic GGUF saving for non-Llama/Mistral models
  - Uses llama.cpp as well

#### C. LoRA Merging Engine:

- `_merge_lora()` (lines 159-187)
  - **Core LoRA weight merging logic**
  - Handles Bitsandbytes 4bit (Bnb_Linear4bit)
  - Handles PEFT LoRA layers (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)
  - Dequantizes 4bit weights to float32
  - Applies LoRA formula: W_merged = W + (s * A^T @ B^T)
  - Preserves bias terms

#### D. Merged Model Saving (Memory Efficient):

Lines 550-760 contain the **merged model creation pipeline**:
- Extracts model dtype from config (float16/bfloat16)
- Iterates through all transformer layers
- For each layer, processes:
  - **LLAMA_WEIGHTS**: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
  - **LLAMA_LAYERNORMS**: input_layernorm, post_attention_layernorm, etc.
- Smart memory management:
  - Tries to keep merged weights in VRAM if space available
  - Falls back to RAM if VRAM full
  - Saves to disk (temporary location) if RAM also full
  - Uses MMAP loading for minimal overhead
- Removes quantization_config from model config (since now merged to float16)
- Saves via internal_model.save_pretrained()

#### E. Adapter Saving (LoRA):

- Simple path through `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`
- Uses standard PEFT save_pretrained
- Stores only LoRA weights (A, B matrices)
- Much smaller file size compared to merged models

#### F. Hub Upload Functions:

- `upload_to_huggingface()` (lines 1495-1585)
  - Handles model card creation
  - Manages tags and metadata
  - Handles authentication and repo creation

- `create_huggingface_repo()` (lines 1456-1494)
  - Creates HF repo if it doesn't exist

#### G. Ollama Integration:

- `create_ollama_modelfile()` (lines 1618-1662)
  - Generates Modelfile for Ollama compatibility

- `push_to_ollama()` (lines 1735-1767)
  - Pushes to Ollama Hub

#### H. Generic Model Support:

- `unsloth_generic_save()` (lines 2356-2410)
  - For non-Llama-based architectures
  
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 2413-2453)
  - Wrapper for generic models
  
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 2456-2500)
  - Hub push for generic models

#### I. TorchAO Quantization:

- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (lines 2502-2563)
  - Saves using TorchAO quantization instead of bitsandbytes

#### J. LoRA to GGML (Legacy):

- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (lines 2143-2210)
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (lines 2211-2275)
  - Legacy LoRA to GGML format conversion

### 3. METHOD PATCHING SYSTEM

**`patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2569-2678)**

This function patches models with save methods:
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged()      # Save merged float16
model.push_to_hub_merged()          # Push merged to HF Hub
model.save_pretrained_gguf()        # Save as GGUF
model.push_to_hub_gguf()            # Push GGUF to Hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml()        # Save as GGML (legacy)
model.push_to_hub_ggml()            # Push GGML (legacy)
model.save_pretrained_torchao()     # Save with TorchAO quantization
```

These are dynamically attached via `types.MethodType` binding.

### 4. KEY CLASSES/TYPES HANDLED

- `Bnb_Linear4bit` - Bitsandbytes 4bit layers
- `Peft_Linear4bit` - PEFT 4bit LoRA layers
- `Peft_Linear` - PEFT float LoRA layers
- `PeftModelForCausalLM` - PEFT wrapper models
- QUANT_STATE - Quantization state from unsloth kernels

### 5. QUANTIZATION METHODS SUPPORTED

All options in ALLOWED_QUANTS dict (lines 79-107):
- **Unquantized**: f32, f16, bf16 (full precision variants)
- **Moderate compression**: q8_0
- **Recommended**: q4_k_m, q5_k_m
- **Light**: q2_k, q3_k_l, q3_k_m, q3_k_s, q3_k_xs
- **Classic**: q4_0, q4_1, q5_0, q5_1
- **Special**: q6_k

### 6. WORKFLOW PATHS

**Path 1: Save LoRA Only**
```
model.save_pretrained_merged(..., save_method="lora")
└─> unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="lora")
    └─> model.save_pretrained() [standard HF save]
```

**Path 2: Save Merged Float16**
```
model.save_pretrained_merged(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
└─> unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
    ├─> Extract dtype from config
    ├─> Loop through layers
    ├─> For each layer: _merge_lora() [merge LoRA weights]
    ├─> Smart memory management (VRAM → RAM → Disk)
    └─> Save via internal_model.save_pretrained()
```

**Path 3: Save as GGUF**
```
model.save_pretrained_gguf(..., quantization_method="q4_k_m")
└─> First: unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
└─> Then: save_to_gguf(...)
    ├─> Install llama.cpp (if needed)
    ├─> python llama.cpp/convert-hf-to-gguf.py [convert to f16 GGUF]
    └─> llama-quantize [quantize to target format]
```

**Path 4: Push Merged to Hub**
```
model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id="...", save_method="merged_16bit")
└─> unsloth_save_model(..., push_to_hub=True)
    ├─> Create repo if needed
    ├─> Merge LoRA weights (same as Path 2)
    └─> Upload files to Hugging Face
```

### 7. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**Memory Management (lines 485-530):**
- Calculates available RAM before saving
- Subtracts shard size (default 5GB)
- Multiplies by maximum_memory_usage ratio
- Logs estimated RAM usage

**LoRA Merging Algorithm (lines 159-187):**
```python
# For each LoRA layer:
W = dequantize(W)  # If 4bit
W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)  # Merge LoRA weights
W = convert_to_dtype(W)  # Back to original dtype
```

**Merged Config Handling (lines 682-692):**
- Removes `quantization_config` from model config (since now float)
- Preserves all other config settings
- Reverts after saving

**Disk Optimization (Kaggle/Colab - lines 539-547):**
- Detects Kaggle/Colab environments
- Removes cached models to free disk space
- Moves temporary files to /tmp

### 8. TEST COVERAGE

Test file: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`

Tests:
- `test_save_merged_16bit()` - Verify float16 merged saving
- `test_save_merged_4bit()` - Verify 4bit merged saving
- `test_save_torchao()` - Verify TorchAO quantization
- `test_save_and_inference_torchao()` - Full inference test

### 9. FILE LOCATIONS

**Primary:** 
- `/unsloth/save.py` - All save/export logic (2,678 lines)

**Supporting:**
- `/unsloth/utils/hf_hub.py` - HF Hub API helpers
- `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` - Tokenizer handling (includes GGUF fixes)
- `/tests/saving/` - Test suite with merged/GGUF/TorchAO tests

### 10. KEY EXPORTED FUNCTIONS

From `__all__` (lines 48-54):
```python
"print_quantization_methods"
"unsloth_save_model"
"save_to_gguf"
"patch_saving_functions"
"create_huggingface_repo"
```

This comprehensive architecture allows Unsloth to provide seamless saving in multiple formats (LoRA, merged float16, GGUF, TorchAO, GGML) while managing memory efficiently and supporting both local saving and HF Hub uploads.

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me create my report:

## Comprehensive Analysis of Unsloth Save/Export Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the save/export system:

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Top-level directory structure:**
```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                  # Main package initialization
├── save.py                      # MAIN: 2,678 lines - All save/export functionality
├── chat_templates.py            # Chat template configurations
├── tokenizer_utils.py           # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                   # Training utilities
├── _auto_install.py             # Auto-installation helpers
├── import_fixes.py              # Import compatibility fixes
├── dataprep/                    # Data preparation modules
├── kernels/                     # Custom CUDA kernels for optimization
├── models/                      # Model implementations (llama, mistral, gemma, qwen, etc.)
├── registry/                    # Model registry
└── utils/
    ├── hf_hub.py               # Hugging Face Hub utilities
    └── __init__.py
```

### 2. SAVE.PY - THE CORE FILE

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines)

This is the monolithic file containing ALL save/export functionality. It includes:

#### A. Main Save Functions (Classes & Methods):

**Core Saving Functions:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main workhorse function (lines 205-760)
  - Handles all save modes: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
  - Manages merged model creation via LoRA weight merging
  - Handles memory management and disk space optimization
  - Supports push_to_hub directly

- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 1321-1361)
  - Wrapper method that calls `unsloth_save_model` with merged mode
  - Merged model saving to float16 (for GGUF compatibility)

- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 1364-1407)
  - Wrapper for pushing merged models to HF Hub

#### B. GGUF Export Functions:

- `save_to_gguf()` (lines 952-1320)
  - **Core GGUF conversion logic** (200+ lines)
  - Manages llama.cpp installation/cloning
  - Handles quantization methods: f16, bf16, f32, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.
  - Converts HF format to GGUF using llama.cpp's convert script
  - Supports multiple quantization levels in one call
  - Handles CPU/GPU availability and fallbacks

- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (lines 1768-1948)
  - **Public API for GGUF export**
  - First converts model to merged_16bit format
  - Installs llama.cpp (blocking or non-blocking)
  - Generates GGUF files with specified quantization
  - Creates Ollama Modelfile alongside GGUF
  - Optional push_to_hub for GGUF files

- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (lines 1951-2128)
  - Pushes GGUF models to Hugging Face Hub
  - Handles GGUF-specific metadata

- `save_to_gguf_generic()` (lines 2276-2352)
  - Generic GGUF saving for non-Llama/Mistral models
  - Uses llama.cpp as well

#### C. LoRA Merging Engine:

- `_merge_lora()` (lines 159-187)
  - **Core LoRA weight merging logic**
  - Handles Bitsandbytes 4bit (Bnb_Linear4bit)
  - Handles PEFT LoRA layers (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)
  - Dequantizes 4bit weights to float32
  - Applies LoRA formula: W_merged = W + (s * A^T @ B^T)
  - Preserves bias terms

#### D. Merged Model Saving (Memory Efficient):

Lines 550-760 contain the **merged model creation pipeline**:
- Extracts model dtype from config (float16/bfloat16)
- Iterates through all transformer layers
- For each layer, processes:
  - **LLAMA_WEIGHTS**: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
  - **LLAMA_LAYERNORMS**: input_layernorm, post_attention_layernorm, etc.
- Smart memory management:
  - Tries to keep merged weights in VRAM if space available
  - Falls back to RAM if VRAM full
  - Saves to disk (temporary location) if RAM also full
  - Uses MMAP loading for minimal overhead
- Removes quantization_config from model config (since now merged to float16)
- Saves via internal_model.save_pretrained()

#### E. Adapter Saving (LoRA):

- Simple path through `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`
- Uses standard PEFT save_pretrained
- Stores only LoRA weights (A, B matrices)
- Much smaller file size compared to merged models

#### F. Hub Upload Functions:

- `upload_to_huggingface()` (lines 1495-1585)
  - Handles model card creation
  - Manages tags and metadata
  - Handles authentication and repo creation

- `create_huggingface_repo()` (lines 1456-1494)
  - Creates HF repo if it doesn't exist

#### G. Ollama Integration:

- `create_ollama_modelfile()` (lines 1618-1662)
  - Generates Modelfile for Ollama compatibility

- `push_to_ollama()` (lines 1735-1767)
  - Pushes to Ollama Hub

#### H. Generic Model Support:

- `unsloth_generic_save()` (lines 2356-2410)
  - For non-Llama-based architectures
  
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 2413-2453)
  - Wrapper for generic models
  
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 2456-2500)
  - Hub push for generic models

#### I. TorchAO Quantization:

- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (lines 2502-2563)
  - Saves using TorchAO quantization instead of bitsandbytes

#### J. LoRA to GGML (Legacy):

- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (lines 2143-2210)
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (lines 2211-2275)
  - Legacy LoRA to GGML format conversion

### 3. METHOD PATCHING SYSTEM

**`patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2569-2678)**

This function patches models with save methods:
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged()      # Save merged float16
model.push_to_hub_merged()          # Push merged to HF Hub
model.save_pretrained_gguf()        # Save as GGUF
model.push_to_hub_gguf()            # Push GGUF to Hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml()        # Save as GGML (legacy)
model.push_to_hub_ggml()            # Push GGML (legacy)
model.save_pretrained_torchao()     # Save with TorchAO quantization
```

These are dynamically attached via `types.MethodType` binding.

### 4. KEY CLASSES/TYPES HANDLED

- `Bnb_Linear4bit` - Bitsandbytes 4bit layers
- `Peft_Linear4bit` - PEFT 4bit LoRA layers
- `Peft_Linear` - PEFT float LoRA layers
- `PeftModelForCausalLM` - PEFT wrapper models
- QUANT_STATE - Quantization state from unsloth kernels

### 5. QUANTIZATION METHODS SUPPORTED

All options in ALLOWED_QUANTS dict (lines 79-107):
- **Unquantized**: f32, f16, bf16 (full precision variants)
- **Moderate compression**: q8_0
- **Recommended**: q4_k_m, q5_k_m
- **Light**: q2_k, q3_k_l, q3_k_m, q3_k_s, q3_k_xs
- **Classic**: q4_0, q4_1, q5_0, q5_1
- **Special**: q6_k

### 6. WORKFLOW PATHS

**Path 1: Save LoRA Only**
```
model.save_pretrained_merged(..., save_method="lora")
└─> unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="lora")
    └─> model.save_pretrained() [standard HF save]
```

**Path 2: Save Merged Float16**
```
model.save_pretrained_merged(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
└─> unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
    ├─> Extract dtype from config
    ├─> Loop through layers
    ├─> For each layer: _merge_lora() [merge LoRA weights]
    ├─> Smart memory management (VRAM → RAM → Disk)
    └─> Save via internal_model.save_pretrained()
```

**Path 3: Save as GGUF**
```
model.save_pretrained_gguf(..., quantization_method="q4_k_m")
└─> First: unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
└─> Then: save_to_gguf(...)
    ├─> Install llama.cpp (if needed)
    ├─> python llama.cpp/convert-hf-to-gguf.py [convert to f16 GGUF]
    └─> llama-quantize [quantize to target format]
```

**Path 4: Push Merged to Hub**
```
model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id="...", save_method="merged_16bit")
└─> unsloth_save_model(..., push_to_hub=True)
    ├─> Create repo if needed
    ├─> Merge LoRA weights (same as Path 2)
    └─> Upload files to Hugging Face
```

### 7. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**Memory Management (lines 485-530):**
- Calculates available RAM before saving
- Subtracts shard size (default 5GB)
- Multiplies by maximum_memory_usage ratio
- Logs estimated RAM usage

**LoRA Merging Algorithm (lines 159-187):**
```python
# For each LoRA layer:
W = dequantize(W)  # If 4bit
W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)  # Merge LoRA weights
W = convert_to_dtype(W)  # Back to original dtype
```

**Merged Config Handling (lines 682-692):**
- Removes `quantization_config` from model config (since now float)
- Preserves all other config settings
- Reverts after saving

**Disk Optimization (Kaggle/Colab - lines 539-547):**
- Detects Kaggle/Colab environments
- Removes cached models to free disk space
- Moves temporary files to /tmp

### 8. TEST COVERAGE

Test file: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`

Tests:
- `test_save_merged_16bit()` - Verify float16 merged saving
- `test_save_merged_4bit()` - Verify 4bit merged saving
- `test_save_torchao()` - Verify TorchAO quantization
- `test_save_and_inference_torchao()` - Full inference test

### 9. FILE LOCATIONS

**Primary:** 
- `/unsloth/save.py` - All save/export logic (2,678 lines)

**Supporting:**
- `/unsloth/utils/hf_hub.py` - HF Hub API helpers
- `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` - Tokenizer handling (includes GGUF fixes)
- `/tests/saving/` - Test suite with merged/GGUF/TorchAO tests

### 10. KEY EXPORTED FUNCTIONS

From `__all__` (lines 48-54):
```python
"print_quantization_methods"
"unsloth_save_model"
"save_to_gguf"
"patch_saving_functions"
"create_huggingface_repo"
```

This comprehensive architecture allows Unsloth to provide seamless saving in multiple formats (LoRA, merged float16, GGUF, TorchAO, GGML) while managing memory efficiently and supporting both local saving and HF Hub uploads.

cat > /tmp/unsloth_exploration_report.md << 'EOF'
# Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

## Overview
The Unsloth library uses a combination of monkey-patching, class-level method registration, and instance-level method attachment to expose public APIs and functionality to users. This document covers the structure and patterns discovered.

---

## 1. Main __init__.py Files and Exports

### Top-level: `/unsloth/__init__.py`
The main entry point performs:
- Device detection (CUDA/HIP/XPU)
- Import order validation (warns if critical modules already imported)
- PYTORCH environment setup and GPU memory optimization
- Loads and patches transformers, TRL, and other dependencies
- Imports from models submodule
- Calls `_patch_trl_trainer()` for backwards compatibility

**Key exports imported:**
- From `models`: `*` (wildcard)
- From `models`: `__version__`
- From `save`: `*` (wildcard)
- From `chat_templates`: `*` (wildcard)
- From `tokenizer_utils`: `*` (wildcard)
- From `trainer`: `*` (wildcard)

### Models Submodule: `/unsloth/models/__init__.py`
Explicitly exports:
```python
from .llama      import FastLlamaModel
from .loader     import FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel
from .mistral    import FastMistralModel
from .qwen2      import FastQwen2Model
from .qwen3      import FastQwen3Model
from .qwen3_moe  import FastQwen3MoeModel
from .granite    import FastGraniteModel
from .falcon_h1  import FastFalconH1Model  # conditional
from .dpo        import PatchDPOTrainer, PatchKTOTrainer
from ._utils     import is_bfloat16_supported, is_vLLM_available, __version__
from .rl         import PatchFastRL, vLLMSamplingParams
```

---

## 2. FastLanguageModel Class Structure

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (line 107)

**Inheritance Hierarchy:**
```
FastLanguageModel (in loader.py)
    ↓ inherits from
FastLlamaModel (in llama.py, line 1778)
```

**Key Design Pattern:**
- `FastLanguageModel` is a **dispatcher class** that routes to specific model implementations
- It inherits from `FastLlamaModel` to provide base functionality
- At runtime, it detects the model type and delegates to specialized classes like:
  - `FastLlamaModel` (for llama models)
  - `FastMistralModel` (for mistral)
  - `FastGemmaModel` (for gemma)
  - `FastGemma2Model` (for gemma2)
  - `FastQwen2Model` (for qwen2)
  - `FastQwen3Model` (for qwen3)
  - `FastQwen3MoeModel` (for qwen3 MoE)
  - `FastModel` (fallback for unsupported models)

**Main Public Methods:**
```python
class FastLanguageModel(FastLlamaModel):
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(
        model_name, max_seq_length, dtype, load_in_4bit, load_in_8bit,
        load_in_16bit, full_finetuning, token, device_map, rope_scaling,
        fix_tokenizer, trust_remote_code, use_gradient_checkpointing,
        resize_model_vocab, revision, use_exact_model_name, offload_embedding,
        fast_inference, gpu_memory_utilization, float8_kv_cache, 
        random_state, max_lora_rank, disable_log_stats, qat_scheme, *args, **kwargs
    )
```

---

## 3. How Methods Are Registered on Models

Unsloth uses **three primary mechanisms** to attach methods to models:

### A. Class-Level Monkey-Patching (in `pre_patch()`)

In `FastLlamaModel.pre_patch()` (line 1781):
```python
# Replacing forward methods on transformer classes
LlamaAttention      .forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaSdpaAttention  .forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaFlashAttention2.forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaDecoderLayer   .forward = LlamaDecoderLayer_fast_forward
LlamaModel          .forward = LlamaModel_fast_forward
LlamaForCausalLM    .forward = CausalLM_fast_forward(LlamaModel_fast_forward_inference)
PeftModelForCausalLM.forward = PeftModel_fast_forward
```

This modifies the **class itself**, affecting all future instances.

### B. Instance-Level Method Attachment (in `from_pretrained()`)

After model is created, methods are attached directly to the instance using `functools.partial` and `types.MethodType`:

**functools.partial** (creates bound partial functions):
```python
# From llama.py line 2232-2233
model.for_training  = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training,  model)
model.for_inference = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_inference, model)
```

**types.MethodType** (binds a function as an instance method):
```python
# From llama.py line 2245
model.generate = types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
```

This allows:
```python
model.for_training()    # Called as instance method
model.for_inference()   # Called as instance method
model.generate(...)     # Called as instance method
```

### C. Model Patcher Pattern

The dispatch system passes a `model_patcher` class to each specialized model's `from_pretrained()`:
```python
# From loader.py line 436
model, tokenizer = dispatch_model.from_pretrained(
    ...
    model_patcher = dispatch_model,
    ...
)
```

This allows each model variant to:
1. Execute `pre_patch()` before loading the model
2. Load and configure the model
3. Execute `post_patch()` after loading
4. Attach instance methods via monkey-patching

---

## 4. Models Directory Structure

The models directory contains:

```
unsloth/models/
├── __init__.py                 # Exports all model classes
├── loader.py                   # FastLanguageModel (dispatcher)
├── _utils.py                   # Shared utility functions
├── vision.py                   # FastBaseModel (base class for vision)
│
├── llama.py                    # FastLlamaModel (most complete)
├── mistral.py                  # FastMistralModel
├── gemma.py                    # FastGemmaModel
├── gemma2.py                   # FastGemma2Model
├── granite.py                  # FastGraniteModel
├── cohere.py                   # FastCohereModel
├── qwen2.py                    # FastQwen2Model
├── qwen3.py                    # FastQwen3Model
├── qwen3_moe.py                # FastQwen3MoeModel
├── falcon_h1.py                # FastFalconH1Model
│
├── rl.py                       # PatchFastRL (RL-specific patches)
├── rl_replacements.py          # RL replacement implementations
├── dpo.py                      # DPO trainer patches
├── mapper.py                   # Model type mapping utilities
├── loader_utils.py             # Helper utilities for loading
└── llama4.py                   # Placeholder or future Llama4
```

---

## 5. Base Classes for Models

### Primary Base: `FastLlamaModel`
Located in `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (line 1778)

**Structure:**
- Not an inheritor of any transformers class
- Acts as a **namespace/provider class** for static methods
- All methods are `@staticmethod`

**Static Methods:**
```python
class FastLlamaModel:
    @staticmethod
    def pre_patch()              # Patches transformer classes before loading
    
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(...)     # Loads model, applies patches, attaches methods
    
    @staticmethod
    def post_patch(model, tokenizer)  # Post-load configuration
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_peft_model(model, ...)    # Configures LoRA for the model
    
    @staticmethod
    def patch_peft_model(model, ...)  # Additional PEFT configuration
    
    @staticmethod
    def for_inference(model)     # Switches model to inference mode
    
    @staticmethod
    def for_training(model, use_gradient_checkpointing)  # Switches to training
```

### Secondary Base: `FastBaseModel`
Located in `/unsloth/models/vision.py` (line 283)

Similar structure to `FastLlamaModel` but designed for multimodal/vision models:
- Handles vision-specific configuration
- Provides `for_inference()` and `for_training()` methods
- Integrates with `FastLlamaModel` for language components

**Hierarchy:**
- `FastModel` (in loader.py) - Fallback for unsupported architectures
  - Uses `FastBaseModel` for vision models
  - Acts as universal dispatcher

---

## 6. Method Registration Flow

### Step-by-Step Process (using Llama as example):

1. **User calls:**
   ```python
   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(model_name)
   ```

2. **Dispatch logic (loader.py):**
   - Detects model type from config
   - Routes to `FastLlamaModel.from_pretrained()`

3. **Pre-patch (llama.py):**
   - `FastLlamaModel.pre_patch()` executes (happens once at import time)
   - Patches transformer classes with fast implementations
   - Example: `LlamaAttention.forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward`

4. **Model loading:**
   - Model is loaded via transformers
   - LoRA is applied if needed via PEFT
   - Quantization config is set

5. **Instance method attachment (llama.py, line 2231-2245):**
   ```python
   # Core methods attached to instance
   model.for_training  = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training,  model)
   model.for_inference = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_inference, model)
   
   # Wrapped generate method
   model._old_generate = model.generate
   model.generate = types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
   ```

6. **Post-patch:**
   - `FastLlamaModel.post_patch()` executes
   - Additional model and tokenizer configuration

7. **Return to user:**
   ```python
   return model, tokenizer
   ```

Now user can call:
```python
model.for_training()   # Built-in Unsloth method
model.for_inference()  # Built-in Unsloth method
model.generate(...)    # Wrapped with Unsloth optimizations
```

---

## 7. Important Patterns for Adding New Methods

### Pattern 1: Static methods on model class
```python
class FastLlamaModel:
    @staticmethod
    def my_new_method(model, *args, **kwargs):
        # Implementation
        pass
```

Then attach to instance:
```python
model.my_new_method = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.my_new_method, model)
```

### Pattern 2: Wrapped functions
```python
def my_fast_function(self, *args, **kwargs):
    # Implementation as if it's a method
    pass

# Attach as method
model.my_method = types.MethodType(my_fast_function, model)
```

### Pattern 3: Class-level patching
```python
LlamaModel.forward = my_fast_forward_function  # Affects all instances
```

---

## 8. Trainer Patching Pattern

Located in `/unsloth/trainer.py`:

- `_patch_trl_trainer()` patches TRL's SFTTrainer for backwards compatibility
- Wraps trainer `__init__` with `_backwards_compatible_trainer()`
- Enables smooth transitions between TRL API versions

This is called at import time (line 269 of `__init__.py`):
```python
_patch_trl_trainer()
```

---

## 9. Export Mechanisms

### Direct exports via __init__.py
- Model classes: `FastLanguageModel`, `FastVisionModel`, `FastModel`, etc.
- Trainer classes: `UnslothTrainer`, `UnslothTrainingArguments`
- Utilities: `is_bfloat16_supported`, `is_vLLM_available`
- RL patches: `PatchFastRL`

### Conditional imports
- `FastGemmaModel` - conditional on transformers >= 4.38
- `FastGemma2Model` - conditional on transformers >= 4.42
- `FastFalconH1Model` - conditional on transformers >= 4.53

### Registry system (optional)
- Located in `/unsloth/registry/`
- Provides model metadata and search functionality
- Not required for basic usage but enables model discovery

---

## 10. Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Key Classes |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `loader.py` | Main entry point, dispatcher | `FastLanguageModel`, `FastModel` |
| `llama.py` | Llama model implementation | `FastLlamaModel` |
| `vision.py` | Vision/multimodal support | `FastBaseModel` |
| `mistral.py` | Mistral optimizations | `FastMistralModel` |
| `qwen*.py` | Qwen model variants | `FastQwen2Model`, etc. |
| `gemma*.py` | Gemma optimizations | `FastGemmaModel`, etc. |
| `rl.py` | RL training patches | `PatchFastRL` |
| `trainer.py` | Trainer integration | `UnslothTrainer` |
| `save.py` | Model saving patches | `patch_saving_functions()` |
| `chat_templates.py` | Chat formatting | Various template functions |

EOF

# UNSLOTH CODEBASE EXPLORATION - COMPREHENSIVE REPORT

## 1. MAIN __init__.py FILES AND EXPORTS

### Top-level /unsloth/__init__.py
- Performs device detection (CUDA/HIP/XPU support)
- Validates import order (warns if transformers, trl, peft already loaded)
- Configures PyTorch memory and environment variables

## 2. FASTLANGUAGEMODEL CLASS STRUCTURE

Location: /unsloth/models/loader.py (line 107)
Inherits from: FastLlamaModel

Purpose: Dispatcher class that routes to specialized model implementations

Route logic (line 308-418):
- model_type == "llama" → dispatch_model = FastLlamaModel
- model_type == "mistral" → dispatch_model = FastMistralModel
- model_type == "gemma" → dispatch_model = FastGemmaModel
- model_type == "gemma2" → dispatch_model = FastGemma2Model
- model_type == "qwen2" → dispatch_model = FastQwen2Model
- model_type == "qwen3" → dispatch_model = FastQwen3Model
- Default → dispatch_model = FastModel (fallback)

Public API (static method):
@staticmethod
def from_pretrained(
    model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
    max_seq_length=2048,
    dtype=None,
    load_in_4bit=True,
    load_in_8bit=False,
    load_in_16bit=False,
    full_finetuning=False,
    token=None,
    device_map="sequential",
    rope_scaling=None,
    fix_tokenizer=True,
    trust_remote_code=False,
    use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
    resize_model_vocab=None,
    revision=None,
    use_exact_model_name=False,
    offload_embedding=False,
    fast_inference=False,
    gpu_memory_utilization=0.5,
    float8_kv_cache=False,
    random_state=3407,
    max_lora_rank=64,
    disable_log_stats=True,
    qat_scheme=None,

## 3. HOW METHODS ARE REGISTERED ON MODELS

### THREE REGISTRATION MECHANISMS:

### A. CLASS-LEVEL MONKEY-PATCHING (in pre_patch())
File: /unsloth/models/llama.py (line 1794-1801)

Modifies transformer classes themselves (affects ALL future instances):
```

LlamaSdpaAttention.forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaFlashAttention2.forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaDecoderLayer.forward = LlamaDecoderLayer_fast_forward
LlamaModel.forward = LlamaModel_fast_forward
LlamaForCausalLM.forward = CausalLM_fast_forward(...)
PeftModelForCausalLM.forward = PeftModel_fast_forward
```

Also replaces rotary embedding classes:
- LlamaRotaryEmbedding
- LlamaLinearScalingRotaryEmbedding

### B. INSTANCE-LEVEL METHOD ATTACHMENT (in from_pretrained())
File: /unsloth/models/llama.py (lines 2232-2245)

Using functools.partial:
```
model.for_training = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training, model)
model.for_inference = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_inference, model)
model.fast_generate_batches = functools.partial(generate_batches, model.vllm_engine)
```

Using types.MethodType:
```
model.generate = types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
```

This allows calling as instance methods:
```
model.for_training()      # Works!
model.for_inference()     # Works!
model.generate(...)       # Works!
```

### C. MODEL PATCHER PATTERN
File: /unsloth/models/loader.py (line 436)

dispatch_model.from_pretrained(
    ...
    model_patcher = dispatch_model,
    ...
)

Each model class (FastLlamaModel, FastMistralModel, etc) has:
1. pre_patch() - Patches transformer classes before loading
2. from_pretrained() - Main loading and configuration
3. post_patch() - Post-load configuration
4. get_peft_model() - LoRA configuration
5. for_inference(model) - Static method for inference setup
6. for_training(model) - Static method for training setup

---

## 4. MODELS DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

/unsloth/models/
├── __init__.py (exports all classes)
├── loader.py (FastLanguageModel dispatcher)
├── _utils.py (shared utilities)
├── vision.py (FastBaseModel for multimodal)
├── llama.py (FastLlamaModel - most complete)
├── mistral.py (FastMistralModel)
├── gemma.py (FastGemmaModel)
├── gemma2.py (FastGemma2Model)
├── granite.py (FastGraniteModel)
├── cohere.py (FastCohereModel - disabled)
├── qwen2.py (FastQwen2Model)
├── qwen3.py (FastQwen3Model)
├── qwen3_moe.py (FastQwen3MoeModel)
├── falcon_h1.py (FastFalconH1Model)
├── rl.py (PatchFastRL)
├── rl_replacements.py (RL implementations)
├── dpo.py (DPO trainer patches)
├── mapper.py (Model type mapping)
├── loader_utils.py (Loading helpers)
└── llama4.py (placeholder)

## 5. BASE CLASSES FOR MODELS

### PRIMARY: FastLlamaModel
File: /unsloth/models/llama.py (line 1778)

Structure: NOT inheriting from transformers classes. Acts as namespace/provider.

All methods are @staticmethod (class-level only, no instance methods):

pre_patch()
  - Patches transformer classes with optimized versions
  - Patches rotary embeddings
  - Called at import time

from_pretrained(model_name, max_seq_length, dtype, ...)
  - Loads model from HF hub
  - Applies LoRA quantization if needed
  - Applies optimizations
  - Attaches methods to instance
  - Returns (model, tokenizer)

post_patch(model, tokenizer)
  - Additional configuration after loading
  - Patches model and tokenizer with utility functions

get_peft_model(model, r, target_modules, lora_alpha, ...)
  - Configures LoRA adapters
  - Returns PEFT-wrapped model

patch_peft_model(model, ...)
  - Additional PEFT configuration for inference

for_inference(model) 
  - Switches model to inference mode
  - Disables gradient checkpointing
  - Sets padding_side = "left"
  - Returns model

for_training(model, use_gradient_checkpointing)
  - Switches model to training mode
  - Enables gradient checkpointing
  - Sets padding_side = "right"
  - Returns model

### SECONDARY: FastBaseModel
File: /unsloth/models/vision.py (line 283)

Similar to FastLlamaModel but for multimodal/vision models:
- Handles vision layers
- Supports finetune_vision_layers, finetune_language_layers params
- get_peft_model() supports vision-specific config
- for_inference() and for_training() methods

### FALLBACK: FastModel
File: /unsloth/models/loader.py

Used for models not in the optimized set:
- Still applies optimizations
- Uses FastBaseModel infrastructure
- More generic approach

---

## 6. METHOD REGISTRATION FLOW (COMPLETE SEQUENCE)

Step 1: User calls
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("unsloth/llama-3-8b-bnb-4bit")

Step 2: Dispatcher detects model type (in loader.py)
- Reads config.json or adapter_config.json
- Identifies model_type (e.g., "llama", "mistral")
- Selects dispatch_model class

Step 3: Pre-patch executed (once at import)
- FastLlamaModel.pre_patch() runs
- Patches LlamaAttention.forward
- Patches LlamaDecoderLayer.forward
- Patches LlamaModel.forward
- Patches LlamaForCausalLM.forward
- Patches PeftModelForCausalLM.forward (if PEFT used)

Step 4: Model loading (in from_pretrained)
- Loads model from HF Hub
- Applies 4bit/8bit quantization
- Applies LoRA if configured
- Sets max_seq_length

Step 5: Instance method attachment (line 2231-2245)
model.for_training = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training, model)
model.for_inference = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_inference, model)
model._old_generate = model.generate
model.generate = types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)

Step 6: Post-patch
- FastLlamaModel.post_patch() runs
- Patches saving functions
- Tokenizer configuration

Step 7: Return
return model, tokenizer

User can now call:
model.for_training()
model.for_inference()
model.generate(...)

---

## 7. PATTERN FOR ADDING METHODS

Example from code (lines 2232-2245 in llama.py):

Pattern A: functools.partial
@staticmethod
def my_method(model, arg1, arg2):
    # implementation
    pass

# Attach to instance:
model.my_method = functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.my_method, model)

# User can call:
model.my_method(arg1, arg2)

Pattern B: types.MethodType
def my_function(self, arg1, arg2):
    # self is bound to model
    # can access self.config, self.generate, etc
    pass

# Attach to instance:
model.my_function = types.MethodType(my_function, model)

# User can call:
model.my_function(arg1, arg2)

Pattern C: Class-level patching
SomeTransformersClass.forward = my_fast_forward
# Affects all instances created after this point

---

## 8. TRAINER PATCHING

File: /unsloth/trainer.py

_patch_trl_trainer() function (lines 215-232)
- Patches all TRL trainer __init__ methods
- Wraps them with _backwards_compatible_trainer()
- Enables compatibility across TRL API versions
- Called at import time (line 269 of __init__.py)

Pattern: Uses exec() to dynamically patch trainer classes:
for x in trl_classes:
    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(...)")

---

## 9. KEY INSIGHTS FOR METHOD REGISTRATION

1. TIMING: Methods can be attached at:
   - Import time (class-level patching in pre_patch)
   - Model load time (instance-level in from_pretrained)

2. SCOPE: Methods affect:
   - Class level: All future instances
   - Instance level: Only that specific model

3. BINDING: Two approaches:
   - functools.partial: For static methods
   - types.MethodType: For functions that need self

4. DISCOVERY: Users access methods as:
   - model.for_training() - registered as partial
   - model.for_inference() - registered as partial
   - model.generate() - wrapped with MethodType
   - model.get_peft_model() - accessed from class (inherited)

5. BACKWARDS COMPATIBILITY:
   - model._old_generate stored for wrapping
   - Original methods accessible if needed
   - Allows clean override and restoration

EOF

├── gemma2.py        ← FastGemma2Model

├── qwen3.py         ← FastQwen3Model
├── qwen3_moe.py     ← FastQwen3MoeModel

├── _utils.py        ← Shared utilities

---

===================

  model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("model-name")
  model = model.get_peft_model()
  model.for_training()
  # ... training ...
  model.for_inference()
  output = model.generate(input_ids)

What happens:
  1. FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained() (dispatcher in loader.py)
  2. Detects model type
  3. Calls dispatch_model.from_pretrained() (e.g., FastLlamaModel)
  4. FastLlamaModel.pre_patch() already executed (class-level patches)
  5. Loads model via transformers + PEFT
  6. Attaches methods:
     - model.for_training = functools.partial(...)
     - model.for_inference = functools.partial(...)
     - model.generate = types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
  7. FastLlamaModel.post_patch() runs
  8. Returns (model, tokenizer)
  
  9. User calls model.for_training()
     → FastLlamaModel.for_training(model) executes
  10. User calls model.for_inference()
      → FastLlamaModel.for_inference(model) executes
  11. User calls model.generate(...)
      → unsloth_fast_generate(model, ...) executes
      → Calls FastLlamaModel.for_inference(model) internally
      → Then calls model._old_generate(...) with optimizations

---

8. KEY PATTERN: functools.partial vs types.MethodType
======================================================

functools.partial(func, arg1):
  - Creates a callable that pre-fills arg1
  - Used for static methods
  - Example: functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training, model)
  - When called: model.for_training() → calls partial()
    → functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training, model)()
    → FastLlamaModel.for_training(model)

types.MethodType(func, instance):
  - Binds a function to an instance as a method
  - Used when function needs self/instance reference
  - Example: types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
  - When called: model.generate(...)
    → unsloth_fast_generate(model, ...)
    → self = model, accesses self.config, self.generate, etc

Both are stored as model attributes:
  model.for_training (is a partial)
  model.generate (is a bound method)
  model.for_inference (is a partial)

---

     - model.for_inference = functools.partial(...)
     - model.generate = types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
  7. FastLlamaModel.post_patch() runs
  8. Returns (model, tokenizer)
  
  9. User calls model.for_training()
     → FastLlamaModel.for_training(model) executes
  10. User calls model.for_inference()
      → FastLlamaModel.for_inference(model) executes
  11. User calls model.generate(...)
      → unsloth_fast_generate(model, ...) executes
      → Calls FastLlamaModel.for_inference(model) internally
      → Then calls model._old_generate(...) with optimizations

---

8. KEY PATTERN: functools.partial vs types.MethodType
======================================================

functools.partial(func, arg1):
  - Creates a callable that pre-fills arg1
  - Used for static methods
  - Example: functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training, model)
  - When called: model.for_training() → calls partial()
    → functools.partial(FastLlamaModel.for_training, model)()
    → FastLlamaModel.for_training(model)

types.MethodType(func, instance):
  - Binds a function to an instance as a method
  - Used when function needs self/instance reference
  - Example: types.MethodType(unsloth_fast_generate, model)
  - When called: model.generate(...)
    → unsloth_fast_generate(model, ...)
    → self = model, accesses self.config, self.generate, etc

Both are stored as model attributes:
  model.for_training (is a partial)
  model.generate (is a bound method)
  model.for_inference (is a partial)

---
